sections.opening: Sit down, my child... the candle is lit and the letters await to reveal their secrets. In the previous lesson we encountered the world of gematria, how numbers are hidden within letters like diamonds within stone. Today we dive deeper into the hidden secrets of the letters themselves — not just their numerical values, but the profound spiritual meanings they carry. Every Hebrew letter is not just a mark on paper, it is a living, breathing creative force. It is form, meaning and energy that connects us directly to the forces of creation. When we study the letters at the Kabbalistic level, we enter a world where every line and every curve carries spiritual information. Today we will learn to listen to the hidden language of the letters.
sections.theoryA:
What is the Kabbalistic meaning of Hebrew letters?

Every Hebrew letter is a creative force that contains the secret of creation within it. In the letter Aleph (א) — the first and fundamental — we see the secret of unity. Aleph represents the ox, a symbol of the primal force of creation, and in its deep meaning — unity with the divine. This is the force that unifies all separateness in the world and returns everything to the primordial source.

Beth (ב) — the second in order — opens the world of duality. It is the house where creation dwells, the place where unity begins to differentiate into multiplicity. Beth represents blessing, for only in the reality of duality can giving and receiving exist. This is the letter that opens the Torah with the word 'Bereshit' — with Beth, creation began.

Gimel (ג) carries within it the secret of loving-kindness and giving. The camel — with its patient nature — represents giving without expectation of reward. In Kabbalah, Gimel is connected to the attribute of loving-kindness in the Sefirah of Chesed, the infinite flow of abundance from above to below. This is the letter of reward — the spiritual reward that comes to those who give without calculation.

Daleth (ד) — the gate of passage — represents humility and openness to receiving divine abundance. The door can be open or closed, and it invites us to choose: are we open to higher energy or are we locked within the material world? In its humility, Daleth teaches us how to be a passage, a bridge between the physical and spiritual.

practice1.instruction: Choose the letter Aleph (א) and gaze at it for three full minutes. Notice its three lines — the central line and the two diagonal lines. Imagine how the central line connects heaven and earth, and the diagonal lines represent opposing forces meeting in perfect balance. Feel the energy of unity flowing from the letter.

sections.theoryB:
How do the letters connect to the Sefirot and Tarot?

Every Hebrew letter is connected to a specific Sefirah in the Tree of Life and to a specific card in the Tarot. This connection is not merely symbolic — it is practical and active. When we work with a particular letter, we awaken within us the energy of the Sefirah connected to it and connect to the corresponding Tarot archetype.

The letter Hey (ה), for example, is connected to the Sefirah of Chochmah and to the Emperor card in Tarot. Its gematria value is 5, a number representing the five senses and revelation in the physical world. Hey is the window through which divine breath enters the world. When we focus on Hey, we open ourselves to receiving spiritual revelations and deep understanding.

Vav (ו) — the sixth letter — connects to the Sefirah of Tiferet, the central Sefirah that balances between Chesed and Gevurah. Vav is being, the axis that connects and balances. In Tarot, Vav is connected to the Lovers card (value 6), representing choice, balance and decision. This being teaches us how to be connectors between all the opposites in life.

Zayin (ז) carries the secret of spiritual power. It is the weapon that fights darkness, but also the source of spiritual sustenance. Zayin is connected to the Sefirah of Netzach, the force that conquers all obstacles. With gematria value 7, it represents Shabbat, the temporal completion that connects to spiritual eternity. This is the letter of holy war — the inner battle between good and evil.

practice2.instruction: Write on paper the letters א-ב-ג-ד-ה at normal speed, and then write them again with extreme slowness — let each pen stroke be made with full awareness. Feel how the slowness deepens your connection to each letter. At the end, compare the sensation that arose from each writing method.

sections.deepDive:
What's not written in the books

Among the hundreds of years of Kabbalah study, very little was spoken about the healing power contained in drawing letters. When we write a Hebrew letter with full awareness and spiritual intention, we are actually channeling energy to that part of the soul connected to the letter. This is a secret that was carefully guarded in certain Kabbalistic families.

The letter Chet (ח) — representing life and fence — contains in its physical form the secret of energetic protection. When we draw Chet in imagination over our heart, it creates a kind of spiritual protective wall. The letter Tet (ט) — the serpent — contains within it the secret of purifying hidden forces. The serpent, as is known, represents both poison and cure.

Yod (י) — the smallest of all letters — contains within it the greatest power. It is the hands of the Creator, the divine spark that exists in everything. When drawing Yod in imagination at different points in the body, it awakens the hidden sparks in those places. This is the secret of the Kabbalistic healing method that has almost been forgotten from the world.

questions.question: What does the letter Aleph (א) represent in Kabbalistic meaning?
options: Unity with the divine and oneness of creation
Beginning of creation only
Physical power of the ox
Divine spark in man
questions.explanation: The letter Aleph primarily represents unity with the divine and the oneness of all creation. The ox is only its external symbol.

questions.question: Why does the Torah open with the letter Beth (ב) and not Aleph?
options: Because Aleph is too holy to use
Because Beth represents the world of duality in which creation exists
Because Beth is the first letter that was created
Because Beth symbolizes the Temple
questions.explanation: The Torah opens with Beth because creation exists in the reality of duality — there is Creator and created. The pure unity of Aleph is before creation.

questions.question: What spiritual power does the letter Gimel (ג) represent?
options: Power of judgment and severity
Power of wisdom and understanding
Power of loving-kindness and giving without expectation
Power of kingdom and manifestation
questions.explanation: Gimel represents the power of loving-kindness and giving without expectation of reward, like the camel that gives its strength without recompense.

questions.question: What is the special meaning of the letter Hey (ה)?
options: It only symbolizes the number 5
It is the window through which divine breath enters
It represents man's house
It is a symbol of earthly kingdom
questions.explanation: Hey is the window through which divine breath and spiritual revelations enter the physical world.

questions.question: How is the letter Yod (י) connected to the power of creation?
options: It is the smallest and therefore weak
It only represents physical hands
It is the hands of the Creator and the divine spark in everything
It is only a symbol of the number 10
questions.explanation: Yod is the smallest of all letters but contains the greatest power — it is the hands of the Creator and the divine spark found in every being.

closing.summary: In today's lesson we discovered the hidden language of Hebrew letters, how each letter carries within it a living and active spiritual force. We learned that letters are not just marks on paper, but creative forces that connect to the Sefirot and Tarot archetypes. We understood how the first letters — Aleph, Beth, Gimel, Daleth and Hey — contain the secrets of unity, duality, loving-kindness, humility and revelation.
closing.wisdom: The letter is a bridge between spirit and matter — in it the divine will and human expression meet.
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we will delve into the connections between different letters and learn the art of Kabbalistic combination...

faq.question: Is there a difference in spiritual power between intentional letters and regular letters?
faq.answer: Yes, letters written with spiritual intention and full awareness carry greater spiritual power than letters written mechanically.

faq.question: How can one feel the energy of letters in daily life?
faq.answer: Begin with slow and conscious writing of letters, look at their physical forms and feel the meaning that arises from each letter.

faq.question: Why do some letters have low values and others high values in gematria?
faq.answer: The values reflect the place of each letter in the hierarchy of creation — from the primal and subtle forces to physical manifestation.

faq.question: Can letters be used for healing?
faq.answer: In Kabbalah there is an ancient tradition of healing by drawing specific letters in imagination on different parts of the body, but this is advanced wisdom requiring deep training.

seo.title: Meaning of Hebrew Letters in Kabbalah - Aleph, Beth, Gimel
seo.description: Discover the spiritual secrets of Hebrew letters in Kabbalah: meaning of Aleph, Beth, Gimel and connection to Sefirot and Tarot. Deep and practical learning.
